query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Reflector', 'Something which throws back heat, light or sound', 'The essay highlights the reflector of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reflexible', 'Capable of being reflected, or thrown back', 'Her reflexible became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reform', 'To put into a new and improved form or condition', 'Critics argued that the plan might reform the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reformer', 'A person devoted to bringing about change', 'The author hoped the new policy would reformer tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Refract', 'To change direction from entering a different medium', 'Critics argued that the plan might refract the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Refractory', 'Hard or impossible to manage', 'Critics argued that the plan might refractory the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Refrain', 'To hold back from an impulse to say or do something', 'Critics argued that the plan might refrain the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Refringent', 'That refracts, refractive', 'The passage presents a refringent portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Refuge', 'A state of safety, protection or shelter', 'The essay highlights the refuge of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Refulgent', 'Resplendent, or shining brightly and radiantly', 'The reviewer described the tone as refulgent, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Refurbish', 'To rebuild or replenish with all new material', 'Critics argued that the plan might refurbish the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Refusal', 'The act of turning something down', 'Her refusal became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Refutation', 'The act of proving a statement or argument to be false', '“His refutation of the claim was backed by strong evidence.”', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Refute', 'To prove a statement or theory to be false; to deny or contradict', 'Critics argued that the plan might refute the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Regal', 'Of or having to do with royalty', 'The author hoped the new policy would regal tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Regale', 'To entertain lavishly or agreeably', 'The author hoped the new policy would regale tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Regalia', 'The decorations or ceremonial clothes of any office or order', 'Scholars noted a striking regalia between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Regality', 'Royalty, sovereignty or kingship', 'The essay highlights the regality of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Regenerate', 'To construct or create anew', 'The author hoped the new policy would regenerate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Regent', 'A person with power to rule in the absence of the sovereign', 'Critics argued that the plan might regent the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Regicide', 'The killing of a king', 'Her regicide became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Regime', 'Mode of rule or management', 'The essay highlights the regime of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Regimen', 'Orderly government, system of order, administration', 'Her regimen became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Regnant', 'Reigning, ruling, currently holding power  [from 15th c ]', 'Her regnant became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Regress', 'To move backwards to an earlier stage, to devolve', 'The author hoped the new policy would regress tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Regression', 'An action of regressing, a return to a previous state', 'The author hoped the new policy would regression tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Regretful', 'Full of feelings of regret, indulging in regrets', 'What seemed regretful at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Regurgitate', 'To throw up or vomit', 'The author hoped the new policy would regurgitate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Rehabilitate', 'To restore to a condition of good health', 'The author hoped the new policy would rehabilitate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reign', 'To exercise sovereign power, or to rule as a monarch', 'Critics argued that the plan might reign the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reimburse', 'To repay money spent on one''s behalf', 'Critics argued that the plan might reimburse the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Rein', 'A strap by which a rider or driver controls a horse', 'The essay highlights the rein of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reinforce', 'To strengthen or support', '“The bridge was reinforced to withstand strong winds.”', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reinstate', 'To restore somebody to a former position or rank', 'The author hoped the new policy would reinstate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reiterate', 'To repeat for emphasis or clarity', '“The coach reiterated the importance of teamwork.”', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reject', 'To refuse to accept, consider, or agree with something', 'Critics argued that the plan might reject the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Rejoin', 'To come again into the company of', 'Over time, the community learned to rejoin its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Rejoinder', 'An answer to a reply', 'Critics argued that the plan might rejoinder the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Rejuvenate', 'To make young again', 'The author hoped the new policy would rejuvenate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Relapse', 'To fall back into a former state', 'Over time, the community learned to relapse its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Relegate', 'To assign to a lower position or rank', '“The player was relegated to the bench after poor performance.”', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Relent', 'To become less severe or intense, to become less hard', 'Critics argued that the plan might relent the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Relevant', 'Directly related, connected, or pertinent to a topic', 'The author hoped the new policy would relevant tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reliable', 'Able to be trusted or depended on; consistently good in quality or performance', 'The author hoped the new policy would reliable tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reliance', 'The act of depending on someone or something, trust', 'Scholars noted a striking reliance between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reliant', 'Having dependence on', 'The passage presents a reliant portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Relic', 'A surviving memorial of something from the past', 'Her relic became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Relinquish', 'To voluntarily cease to keep or claim', 'Critics argued that the plan might relinquish the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Reliquary', 'A container to hold or display religious relics', 'Over time, the community learned to reliquary its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Relish', 'Liking or enjoyment of the taste of something', 'The essay highlights the relish of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
